mission.title: contour: Add support for external client certificate validation (mTLS)
mission.description: Add support for external client certificate validation (mTLS). Community-requested feature.

steps.title: Check current contour deployment
steps.description:
Verify your contour version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n contour -l app.kubernetes.io/name=contour
helm list -n contour 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working contour installation.

steps.title: Review contour configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant contour configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n contour -l app.kubernetes.io/name=contour
kubectl get configmap -n contour -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=contour
```
Enable client certificate validation for external clients ([mTLS](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mutual_authentication))

Since mTLS is done for a virtual server, not a path, annotations in service manifests can not be used.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Add support for external client certificate validation…
steps.description:
The client certificate is not forwarded from Envoy to the backend POD (I got that wrong) but envoy should forward client certificate details
```yaml
apiVersion: contour.heptio.com/v1beta1
kind: IngressRoute
metadata:
  name: www
  namespace: default
spec:
  virtualhost:
    fqdn: foo.com
    tls:
      secretName: server-secret
      clientValidation:
        secretName: client-secret
  routes:
    - match: /
      services:
        - name: s1
          port: 80
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n contour -l app.kubernetes.io/name=contour
kubectl get events -n contour --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Add support for external client certificate validation…" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
The client certificate is not forwarded from Envoy to the backend POD (I got that wrong) but envoy should forward client certificate details ([doc](https://www.envoyproxy.io/docs/envoy/latest/api-v2/config/filter/network/http_connection_manager/v2/http_connection_manager.proto#envoy-api-enum-config-filter-network-http-connection-manager-v2-httpconnectionmanager-forwardclientcertdetails)); 

```
codeSnippets:
apiVersion: contour.heptio.com/v1beta1
kind: IngressRoute
metadata:
  name: www
  namespace: default
spec:
  virtualhost:
    fqdn: foo.com
    tls:
      secretName: server-secret
      clientValidation:
        secretName: client-secret
  routes:
    - match: /
      services:
        - name: s1
          port: 80

tls_context:
        require_client_certificate: true
        common_tls_context:
          validation_context:
            verify_certificate_spki: j1fFFSBuF7S/Hzk5xsIq7N7wvVm8xdMpMnt0OUsz5rs=

openssl x509 -in default/cert/kahttp.crt -noout -pubkey \
  | openssl pkey -pubin -outform DER \
  | openssl dgst -sha256 -binary \
  | openssl enc -base64

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with contour installed or the issue environment reproducible.
